Source: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-457/.pan/review/agent-pan-457-review-5c799040/synthesis.md

## Required action

Fix every blocking review finding, commit the fixes, then re-request review with:

`pan review request PAN-457 -m "Fixed review issues"`

TESTS FAILED for PAN-457:

Quality gates (typecheck, lint, 3689 tests) all pass.

UAT FAILURE — GET /api/discovered-sessions/:id returns HTTP 500 (`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'id')`). Root cause: src/dashboard/server/routes/discovered-sessions.ts lines 184 and 207 access `req.params as { id?: string }` to extract the route parameter, but the correct Effect HTTP pattern in this codebase is `yield* HttpRouter.params`. The `req.params` property is undefined on the HttpServerRequest object, causing a null-deref on every parameterised session request. This breaks two routes: `GET /api/discovered-sessions/:id` (session detail fetch, triggered by clicking any session row) and `POST /api/discovered-sessions/:id/enrich` (single-session enrichment). All other routes in the same file that do NOT use `req.params` (list, scan, batch-enrich, stats, search) work correctly. The unit tests for this path test the DB function directly (`getDiscoveredSessionById`), not the HTTP handler, so the route bug was not caught by the test suite.

Additional: console warning `[EventRouter] replay failed: Missing key at ["value"][32]["payload"]["issueId"]` on server startup — appears pre-existing (EventRouter.tsx unchanged in this branch, main server uses same event store without known crash).

Fix required: replace `req.params as { id?: string }` with `yield* HttpRouter.params` at lines 184 and 207 of src/dashboard/server/routes/discovered-sessions.ts.

## REQUIRED: Fix ALL test failures, then invoke the /rebase-and-submit skill

1. Read each test failure carefully
2. Fix the code causing EVERY failure
3. Run the test suite locally to verify your fixes pass
4. Commit every change
5. Invoke the /rebase-and-submit skill for PAN-457 — this is an atomic task that runs pan done (which handles rebase + push + re-submit internally)

Do NOT stop between steps. Do NOT run git push manually — the skill handles it. Do NOT stop until pan done has completed successfully.

2. Fix the code causing the failure
3. Run the failing check locally to verify it passes
